description: Scanning electron micrograph displaying rows of nano-indentation imprints arranged in a grid pattern across different phases in Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels. The triangular Berkovich indents were produced using a constant loading/unloading rate of 500 μN s⁻¹ to a maximum load of 10000 μN with a 20 s hold time, as stated in the caption and methods. The image captures indentations performed on various microstructural constituents including solution-treated γ and α phases, B2 stringer bands, micro-meter sized B2 particles, and nano-disk B2 precipitates, as well as composite regions such as γ + cuboid κ and α + needle-shape κ. The scale bar indicates 2 μm. This figure illustrates the experimental arrangement for spatially resolved hardness measurements, where arrays of 50-70 indentations were applied across regions of interest to statistically determine the nanomechanical properties of individual phases and phase combinations, supporting the investigation of how annealing temperature and Ni addition affect the hardness of κ-carbides and B2 intermetallics in these duplex steels.
